2009-06-26.title: FIA-FOTA War Not Quite Finished
2009-06-26.abstract: Yes, well, as I stated in my previous post, I was not entirely convinced that Max Mosley would really be gone. After all, we have been through this sort of thing before – when he announced his retirement a few years ago and then returned “by popular demand.” Autosport is now reporting that Max is not happy with thing FOTA has been saying and he is saying he may be back after all. So obviously, for all those people who commented on the previous post and were happy about the prospect of a breakaway series, well, looks like maybe your wish could still be granted. See if you can make any sense out of the import of this report by Autosport, and previous to that on racefax.com. Or if you’re like me, you might want to think of something else for a few days and see if someone intercedes again and settles this problem before it begins to get out of hand….

2009-06-27.abstract: The focus of the most popular entertainment show on Argentine television Thursday was Néstor Kirchner, the former president now running for Congress.

2009-06-27.title: Eyes on Europe, Albanians Prepare to Vote
2009-06-27.abstract: Albanians, feeling increasingly isolated, will vote in parliamentary elections that many hope could help integrate the country with the West.

2009-06-28.title: War Hero in Vietnam Forces Government to Listen
2009-06-28.abstract: Gen. Vo Nguyen Giap is challenging the secretive workings of the Communist government over a mining operation run by a Chinese company.

2009-06-28.title: U.S. Commander Says Iraq Forces Ready
2009-06-28.abstract: Gen. Ray Odierno, the American commander in Iraq, spoke ahead of Tuesday’s deadline for the withdrawal of American combat troops from Iraq’s cities and towns.

2009-06-28.title: New Cancer Treatment Shows Promise in Testing
2009-06-28.abstract: A new method of attacking cancer cells, developed by researchers in Australia, has proved surprisingly effective in animal tests.

2009-06-28.title: Honduran President Is Ousted in Coup
2009-06-28.abstract: After President José Manuel Zelaya was ousted by the army on Sunday, the Honduran Congress chose Roberto Micheletti to be the new president.

2009-06-28.title: Iran Arrests Iranian Employees of British Embassy as Protests Return
2009-06-28.abstract: The government arrested nine Iranian employees of the British Embassy, while the police in Tehran beat and fired tear gas at several thousand protesters.

2009-06-29.title: Exiled by Russia: Casinos and Jobs
2009-06-29.abstract: Russia is closing every legal casino this week, throwing hundreds of thousands of people out of work.

2009-06-29.title: Persian Station in Britain Rattles Officials in Iran
2009-06-29.abstract: The BBC’s Persian-language channel has been singled out as the main threat among foreign news broadcasters for what the Iranian government calls biased coverage.

2009-06-29.title: Rare Hemisphere Unity in Assailing Honduran Coup
2009-06-29.abstract: The responses from governments both revealed and disguised fissures over different forms of democratic government that are taking root in the region.

2009-06-29.title: Israel Said to Be Open to Settlement Freeze
2009-06-29.abstract: While such an offer would fall short of the American demand that all building be halted, it shows that pressure from Washington may be having an effect.

2009-06-29.title: A Whiff of Controversy and South African Wines
2009-06-29.abstract: After one critic’s comment associating a “burnt rubber” odor with South African reds, a scientific team has been working to locate the possible source.

2009-06-30.title: U.S. Resumes Surveillance Flights Over Pakistan
2009-06-30.abstract: Information is being shared with Pakistani commanders fighting militants near the Afghan border.

2009-06-30.title: Rural South Koreans’ Global Links Grow, Nourished by a Satellite Crop
2009-06-30.abstract: Lee Si-kap's enthusiasm for satellite dishes has helped to provide a valuable link between the community of foreign brides in South Korea and their homelands.

2009-06-30.title: For a Post in Europe, a Renaissance Admiral
2009-06-30.abstract: Adm. James G. Stavridis will become the new American and NATO commander in Europe.

2009-06-30.title: French Continue Search for Clues to Jet’s Loss
2009-06-30.abstract: Ships off the Brazilian coast are seeking the voice and data recorders from Air France Flight 447, which went down June 1.

2009-06-30.title: Zimbabwe: Political Partnership Shows Further Strain
2009-06-30.abstract: Taking a harder line on its power-sharing deal with President Robert Mugabe’s ZANU-PF party, the opposition party boycotted a cabinet meeting on Monday.
